image_dependency: question_text explicitly says the transition-state structure is shown in the image
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the key evidence is visual
all four choices are similar SMILES strings, so selecting the answer depends on reading fine-grained structural details from the image
multi_step: solver must parse the depicted molecular structure and then map it to one of four candidate SMILES strings
distractors are structurally close variants, requiring comparison of ring connectivity/atom placement rather than simple lookup
the instruction to ignore arrows removes one visual element but still leaves nontrivial structure-to-string matching
verifiability: answer format is short_text and exactly matches one provided multiple-choice option
registry_passed=true, image exists, and answer/question are both present
final correctness can be checked deterministically by exact option/string match even if the reasoning is image-based

decision_reasons: Question, answer, and image are all present, so the core semantics are likely still recoverable.
The item is highly image-dependent and requires fine-grained chemical structure disambiguation, which raises extraction difficulty.
The available image is small (232x226), creating a readability risk for the visual details needed to distinguish similar answer choices.
